meta.title: Moody's / Team8: Cyber Risk Ratings for Companies
meta.description:
Derek Vadala of Cyber Assessments, a joint venture with Moody's and Team8, discusses the early efforts to create a Cyber Risk rating system for an organization. Moody's is a natural fit to do this as they provide Credit Ratings (eg AAA, Ba1) that are relied on my the world.

These ratings could be used for a variety of purposes. The obvious one for Moody's is a component of their Credit Ratings. However it also could be used as part of an insurance product, by a vendor or asset owner to consider a supply chain partner, or for board/executive understanding of where they stand as compared to peers.

Derek explains three of the challenges they are facing. The one I find most interesting is the fidelity vs. effort tradeoff and decision.

Interestingly Moody's thinks it is important that they do not provide remediation services so there is true independence.
